TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr-puratu_Muu

S.FORM: 'una kani mamarang kanakanavu miana.
S.TRANSL: 據說以前有卡那卡那富的老人家。
It is said that there was an old Kanakanavu person.

S.FORM: sua kari iisi ia manasʉ koo pa kam tunmana.
S.TRANSL: 這個話，或許你們未曾聽過。
As for this story, perhaps you haven't heard about it.

S.FORM: te ku poisua paraa'ʉna masiin poisua 'inia.
S.TRANSL: 現在我要聊一聊關於...說關於那個（的事情）。
Now I want to chat about...say about that.

S.FORM: makasi kan sua kʉkʉnangiin tanama teen para kicacʉ sua cau iisi misa kan.
S.TRANSL: 他的同伴說：「（我們）試著嚇嚇這個人。」
His partner said, "Let's try to frighten this person."

S.FORM: ʉʉ'ʉ misa kan taciin.
S.TRANSL: 另外一個人說「好啊。」
Another person said "OK."

S.FORM: parakicacʉ kan nia.
S.TRANSL: 「嚇那一位」
"frighten that (man)"

S.FORM: 'a'a te ci kita makenanu.
S.TRANSL: 「啊，我們要如何啦？」
"Ah, how should we do?"

S.FORM: makasua cu sua cau taciin isua.
S.TRANSL: 那一個人（另外一個人）就那樣。
That man (the other) was in that way.

S.FORM: 'ʉʉ nakai miaa maka'uru ia 'una kan 'ʉʉ sua 'utin ni ia 'ʉʉ tupuruiin kan nia takipʉnʉn kan kee sua 'utin ni na ʉnʉ'ʉnai.
S.TRANSL: 首先，有他的生殖器，如果坐著，他的生殖器就會盤繞在地上。
At first, there was his genital; if he sat, his genital would coil on the ground.

S.FORM: noo makasiin kan cau tuu te ku mosiparʉ 'akia tapeca misa kani ia.
S.TRANSL: 我想要涉水到對岸，（但說）沒有通道。
I wanted to go to the opposite bank through water, (but he) said there was no passage.

S.FORM: arisʉ'ʉrʉn kan kee 'utini mari marikusa sipara.
S.TRANSL: 他拉著他的生殖器到對岸。
He pulled his genital to the opposite bank.

S.FORM: makasua cu aranee cu nia taniuru'e cu kan cau tumani'ura e rʉmʉra.
S.TRANSL: 人們就在那裡給他搗蛋。
The persons then acted up there (on his genital).

S.FORM: 'a ta mo'ʉna kita 'una etia miakaka kitana cau misa kan.
S.TRANSL: 「啊，走吧，我們逃離，有人正要傷害我們。」
"Ah, let's go, let's escape, there is someone wanting to damage us."

S.FORM: makasua cu te kan manguru.
S.TRANSL: 就這樣，要逃跑了。
That way, (we) wanted to escape.

S.FORM: ariupupange kan mamia nia sii nimanguru cu cucumeen.
S.TRANSL: 他們只能在那邊慌亂，因為別人都已經逃走了。
They had no choice but to lose their head there, becuase (all) others already escaped.

S.FORM: kan makai iikia sua makahua naturu manasʉ tananiin pacʉpʉcʉpʉʉng makahua si makai iikia 'akia tavarʉ'een.
S.TRANSL: 像我一樣呆呆的，沒有他所知道的。
Like me, who was silly and didn't have something I knew.

S.FORM: sua 'utini ia te cu kan takukingti.
S.TRANSL: His wanted to pull the thorn away from his genital.
他的生殖器要拔刺。

S.FORM: makasi kan makahi kan tu te maku te ku poso'uma takukingti 'utin aku misee kan.
S.TRANSL: 他說：「我要用我的生殖器作為代價來請人工作。」
He said, "I wanted to ask someone to work at the cost of my genital."

S.FORM: nakai tia naannakʉ kan kavangvang 'aaka kan saronee.
S.TRANSL: 但是都要是女性，男人不行。
But all should be women, man are not OK.

S.FORM: makahua cu mara kan toomarU umara kan toomarU tia takukingti.
S.TRANSL: 他拿簸箕要拔刺。
He took a bamboo container in order to pull out the thorns.

S.FORM: sua tia tasUU'a kingti 'utini ia te cu kan kuratumuru toomarU.
S.TRANSL: 那個簸箕裡刺將要滿了。
The bamboo container was going to be full of thorns.

S.FORM: atii 'utiinpai kUcU mananu mamanUUng maa natuuru misi kan.
S.TRANSL: 「什麼生殖器啦？（這樣）好嗎？」（她們）說：「呆瓜啊。」
"What on earth is that genital? Is it OK?" (they) said, "idiot."

S.FORM: macaca kan saronai isua saronai kan isua 'a'ah.
S.TRANSL: 那個男人笑出來。呸！
That man laughed. Ah!

S.FORM: 'akuun maa pokarikari taku ku tamaiara takukingti sii.
S.TRANSL: 「不要講話，只要勤勞地拔刺，因為...」
"Don't talk and pull out the thorns alone, because..."

S.FORM: uma'UngUrU aiakU aiakU misi kan 'esi uma'UngUrU.
S.TRANSL: 他呻吟著「唉唷唉唷」，（一直）在呻吟。
He moaned, "ouch ouch"; he (kept) moaning.

S.FORM: 'esi kan araamUn nannakU sua kingti 'utini umarUn kan makahi.
S.TRANSL: 女孩子在撿生殖器上的刺。
The women were picking the thorns on the genital.

S.FORM: te cu kan kuratumuru kan toomaru sua kingti nimoon na 'utini.
S.TRANSL: 曾經在生殖器上的刺快裝滿簸箕了。
The bamboo container was going to be filled with the thorn once on the genital.

S.FORM: makasua cu kan po'U nimepacai kan tutui nimepacai kan tutui posoo'uma takukingti 'utini.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣，已經殺過豬來請人拔生殖器的刺。
That way, (he) had had others pull out the thorns on genitals with the reward of killed pigs.

S.FORM: makasua sua poisua isua.
S.TRANSL: 那個（故事）就是那樣。
That (story) is like that.

S.FORM: 'una pu ku tia poisua ucan sua ucani te muku pocuvUUn 'inia ia.
S.TRANSL: 我還有一個要講的（故事），緊接著（第一個故事）。
I also have one (story) to talk about, immediately followed by (the first story).

S.FORM: 'una kan er manasU sua nganaiin ia manasU puratu kan.
S.TRANSL: 他的名字叫Puratu。
His name is Puratu.

S.FORM: puratu kan hua nganaiin.
S.TRANSL: Puratu是他的名字。
Puratu is his name.

S.FORM: nakai ka'aan koo kia amanUUng mamarang miana sii.
S.TRANSL: 但是，不，我未曾聽清楚老人家（說的），因為...
But, no, I haven't got what the old people had said, because...

S.FORM: makasua nganaiin misee.
S.TRANSL: （人家）說他的名字是那樣。
(People) said that his name is like that.

S.FORM: nikican nikicancan kan cau mamUmUUkU kan sua mamarang cucumeen.
S.TRANSL: （Puratu）跟其他的（老）人一起走，老人家覺得很困擾。
(Puratu) walked with other (old) people, the old people felt confused.

S.FORM: anaka nimikiananu sua cau iisi misa kan po'uru'uruun kan kee 'apacU'UUra anuka.
S.TRANSL: （他們）讓他先看腳印。
(They) made him see the footprint first.

S.FORM: cumUreen kan anuka vutuun vavuru ta'uurong takuuisi ia arapapining kan kee vava na vavuniin.
S.TRANSL: 如果看到鹿、山豬、山羌、山羊的腳印，他就從他的胸袋裡面拿肋骨出來。
If (he) saw the footprints of deer, boars, muntjac deer, and goats, he would take a rib from bag.

S.FORM: makasia mamia cacana.
S.TRANSL: 長度只有這樣。
The length was only like this.

S.FORM: ecu'uun kan kee nia makasi.
S.TRANSL: 他就這樣指著。
He pointed this way.

S.FORM: parirUmaan cu kan kee cau pari parivinoo ngUmUUtoo kisUUn kan kee.
S.TRANSL: 他就使喚別人，跟他說「跟上去找回來」。
He commanded and said to others, "follow and find it back."

S.FORM: UU mamUmUUkU kan cau koo pe mupanaa'U sua cau.
S.TRANSL: 人們很困惑；人們都還沒有射擊。
The people got confused; they hadn't shoot yet.

S.FORM: ati upeen ci niara kee misa kan ucuein maa anuka urupaca kan vava.
S.TRANSL: 他（們）說：究竟他獵到了多少？他只是用肋骨指著腳印。
He (they) said how many on earth he hunted? What he did was simply to aim at the footprints with a rib.

S.FORM: nUtUU'Un kan cau 'inia vavuru tinakuviiri.
S.TRANSL: 人們找到了；山豬在那裡倒下了。
The people found it; a boar fell down there.

S.FORM: kangvang marakurakurape ia makasua kan.
S.TRANSL: 所有的獸類都是那樣。
All animals were like that.

S.FORM: UU makarikari kan ha mamarang.
S.TRANSL: 老人家就議論著。
The old people discussed (it).

S.FORM: kUcU misa sua tacin mariivari.
S.TRANSL: 一個人說：「好啊！」
One man said, "OK!"

S.FORM: makasua cu kapapa'ici KAN kapapa'ici.
S.TRANSL: 就那樣，他們就去釀酒。
So that they brewed wine.

S.FORM: mamanUUng cu pa'ici te cu imUUn.
S.TRANSL: 酒已經好了，可以喝了。
The wine was already good and can be drunk.

S.FORM: 'apimee 'inia.
S.TRANSL: 就給他喝。
He was made to drink.

S.FORM: makai tia sua nganaiin ia puratu tia misee makai puratu misee.
S.TRANSL: 據說他的名字好像是Puratu，叫做Puratu。
It is said that his name seems to be Puratu, (he is) called Puratu.

S.FORM: sii sua sua tavara'an mia ia sua puratu ia tamu na 'Amiu 'Una cu kuei manasU makasua.
S.TRANSL: 因為我們所知道的Puratu據說是'Amiu 'Una的祖父；或許是那樣。
Because Puratu who we know is said to be the grandfather of 'Amiu 'Una; it was perhaps like this.

S.FORM: 'apemee nia sua puratu ia a puratu ia manava kan mamia.
S.TRANSL: 他讓Puratu喝酒，Puratu酒醉了。
He made Puratu drink wine, and Puratu was drunk.

S.FORM: sii sua miana iikita kanakanaku ia vavuni mamia araave 'akia vankavuuka miana.
S.TRANSL: 因為以前我們卡那卡那富只有胸袋和遮襠褲，以前沒有長褲。
Because we Kanakanavu only have chest bags and loincloths in the past; there were no pants previously.

S.FORM: makasua sua iikita kanakanavu miana.
S.TRANSL: 以前我們卡那卡那富就是那樣。
We Kanakanavu was like that in the past.

S.FORM: iimo nuu tupuuru kia tupepe ia kan arapipining sua ecuru.
S.TRANSL: 因此如果光是坐著（不遮掩的話），陰囊就會跑出來。
Therefore, if (one) only sat (without covering it with something), the srotum would be visible.

S.FORM: mitatitiin sua ecuru.
S.TRANSL: 陰囊一直擺盪著。
The scrotium kept swinging.

S.FORM: marava kani marapunuui.
S.TRANSL: （他）就醉倒了。
(He) was drunk and passed out.

S.FORM: 'una kan tacin putuvurukoo cu movovovuniin misa kan.
S.TRANSL: 有一個人說：「去打開他的胸袋。」
Someone said, "Open his chest bag."

S.FORM: putUvurUU kee kan nia vavoniin makahi.
S.TRANSL: 他如此開著他的胸袋。
He opened his chest bag this way.

S.FORM: 'esi kan nipuca na siimpU angici sua vava makasia kan cacana.
S.TRANSL: 包在皮裡面的是這樣長的肋骨。
What was packed in the hide is a rib with such a length.

S.FORM: aree kan nia maripipining makasi putu'upucee kan nia.
S.TRANSL: 他就把東西這樣子拿出來，打開（包裹的東西）。
He took out the object, opening (what was packed).

S.FORM: a'ai makasia naava siaropaceen.
S.TRANSL: 「啊，他的工具原來是這樣。」
"Ah, so that's how his tool is."

S.FORM: vava vava va tutui misa kan.
S.TRANSL: 「原來是家豬的肋骨，」（人們）說。
"So it's a rib of pigs," (people) said.

S.FORM: aree ha cau tumaniooru tumeen.
S.TRANSL: （有）人拿了（肋骨），把它搗蛋丟掉了。
(Some)one took it, doing mischief and throwing it away.

S.FORM: UU maka nureen kan ara nureen cu.
aratakaarU cu 'una kan aratakaarU cu misee kan nia na sua na puratu.
S.TRANSL: 「起床了，」有（人）向他，Puratu，說「起床了」。
"Wake up," there was (someone) saying "wake up" to him, Puratu.

S.FORM: aratakaarU kan su aratakaarU ?? putuvurUUke kan nia vavoniin.
S.TRANSL: （當他）起來時，（他）打開他的胸袋。
(When) working up, (he) opened his chest bag.

S.FORM: makahi anci nasiarupacaa maku misa kan.
S.TRANSL: （他）說：「我原本使用的工具到哪裡了？」
(He) said, "Where does the tool I originally used go?"

S.FORM: nipapining kee sua naserupaceen mecuu'u makasia maa cacana vava.
S.TRANSL: 他拿出來他用來瞄準的肋骨，只剩下這樣長。
He took out the rib he had originally used to aim, whose length was only like this.

S.FORM: a te ci kia makananu misa kan.
S.TRANSL: （他）說：「啊！我要如何？」
(He) said, "Ah! How should I do?"

S.FORM: iimo iikita cau mamane ia 'akuun makasua apaco'e noo neen makasua seropaca cau ia.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們人在世間的時候，無論人們使用什麼，（都）不要那樣做。
Therefore, when we people are in the human world, no matter what people use, never behave like that.

S.FORM: teenooman rUpUUngUn.
S.TRANSL: 忌妒有什麼用呢？
What (can we get) by envying?

S.FORM: tumu'uru kisaa taurhou puyaokuanta teenooman makahua.
S.TRANSL: （這就是）中文所說的「不要管他」。那樣（管他）有什麼用呢？
(That is) so-called "Don't care about him." in Chinese. What (can we get) by doing that?

S.FORM: womenpuyaokuantara kisaa taurhou tadedongsishitadedongsi.
S.TRANSL: （這就是）中文說的「不要管他，他的東西是他的東西。」
(This is) so-called "Let's not care about him. His objects are his objects."

S.FORM: nonoomaniin ia nonoomaniin misa kita mamia.
S.TRANSL: 他的東西是他的東西，我們只要（這樣）想。
His objects are his objects, all we have to do is think (like this).

S.FORM: 'akuun sua tumaniooru aaka makasua.
S.TRANSL: 不要搗蛋，那樣是不好的。
Don't do mischief, that is no good.

S.FORM: iimo iikita kanakanavu kisaa mamarang a 'akuuni tumanioorUU'U.
S.TRANSL: 所以，（就像）我們卡那卡那富老人家所說的，不要搗蛋。
Therefore, (just like) what we Kanakanavu old people say, don't do mischief.

S.FORM: te tia sinpakanooman sua tia tumatianarUU'U cau kUkUUnang.
S.TRANSL: 搗蛋夥伴們有什麼意義？
What is it for to do mischief to partners?

S.FORM: kan tia araaka te kita aaka kangvang tia aaka kangvang nguani misee.
S.TRANSL: 要（這樣）想，我們都會死，他們也會死。
Think that way, we will all die, they will also die.

S.FORM: makasua sua wipakarikari maku.
S.TRANSL: 我說的是那樣。
What I say is like that.

S.FORM: sintuturu maku kamua mamaan tati'ing mo noo atUnU kamu iisi ia 'akuun ruem.
S.TRANSL: 我曾經告訴你們後輩的，如果你們記住這個的話就不要忘記。
What I told you children, if you remember this then don't forget.

S.FORM: pilushuonimen ting ting zekehua gueduepuiaowanggi.
S.TRANSL: 比如說你們聽過這個話，絕對不要忘記。
For example, when you heard this word, don't ever forget (it).

S.FORM: 'akuun ruem kari iisi.
S.TRANSL: 不要忘記這個話。
Don't forget this word.

S.FORM: makasua sua kari maku tumatuturu kamua mamaan maku.
S.TRANSL: 我的話是那樣，我一再地告訴你們，我的孩子們。
My words are like that, I tell you, my children, again and again.

S.FORM: makasua makasua nav misee.
S.TRANSL: （後輩會）說：「原來是那樣。」
(The children would) say, "It's that way."

S.FORM: makasua sua sintuturu kamua cuma Mu'u.
S.TRANSL: Mu'u叔叔告訴你們的就是那樣。
What Uncle Mu'u told you is like that.

S.FORM: necU'Ure iiku mahin cuma Mu'u mahin ia caniava kaisisi kita cakUrU mikoon misee ia 'akia tavarU'U 'akia kita tavarU'U pokarikari.
S.TRANSL: 你看，我Mu'u叔叔現在，比如說我們在聚會所祭祀米貢，我們沒有人會說（祭詞）。
You see, now I, Uncle Mu'u, suppose we are holding the millet ceremony in the assembly hall, none of us can speak the (prayer).

S.FORM: neci'ire nate cu 'e cu ku mamUmUUkU mahin.
S.TRANSL: 你看，我原本要......，我現在已經不知所措了。
Look, I originally wanted... I am now at a loss.

S.FORM: tututuvuusu mamia mamarang mita ia.
S.TRANSL: 我們的老人家只能孤零零的。
Our old people can only be solitary.

S.FORM: neen tia tumatuturu kitana pakacaree kita curu.
S.TRANSL: 誰要告訴我們（要怎麼辦）？我們真的很可憐。
Who is going to tell us (what to do)? We are really poor.

S.FORM: misa kita maa pacUpUcUUpUng.
S.TRANSL: 我們只要（這樣）想。
All we have to do is think (this way).

S.FORM: makasua sua setututuru cuma Mu'u kamua.
S.TRANSL: Mu'u叔叔要對你們說的就是那樣。
What Uncle Mu'u wants to tell you is like that.

S.FORM: 'akuune'e rue ruemee.
S.TRANSL: 絕對不要忘記那個。
Never forget that.

S.FORM: UsU'U na tUkU tuumana masi ma aramamanUng kita.
S.TRANSL: 要放進耳朵，（好好）聽才會變好。
Place (the words) into the ears, listen carefully so that you may become good.

S.FORM: mata sua 'aka cUpUng cuma Mu'u mahin ia mUU cucuru iikita miana ia ia'ah 'una cinmUUra makai kita kurai.
S.TRANSL: Mu'u叔叔現在很憂慮，我們以前真的，哇！據說有人看過我們就像蟲（一樣多）。
Uncle Mu'u is now very anxious; we indeed used to... Wow! Reportedly we were seen to be (as many as) worms.

S.FORM: neen vanai kita KANa kanavu ara mara 'akia sii miana ia cucuru sua iikita KANa kanavu ia nikarU lipUUng 'apepace karavung pakisia.
S.TRANSL: 我們卡那卡那富什麼原因比較沒有（人），因為以往我們卡那卡那富（說）真的，被日本人叫去殺閩南人的牛。
For some unknown reasons we KANa kanavu are few, because we KANa kanavu were, frankly speaking, are made by Japanese to kill oxen of the south Fujian people.

S.FORM: iimo kisa sua iikita ia natia taivu natia taivulan 'una natia taivulan mihee kitana.
S.TRANSL: 所以有人說，我們是殺牛人。
So there are people saying that we are the people who kill oxen.

S.FORM: taivu taivulan misee maa misee kitana sii nimepacai kita karavung.
S.TRANSL: 只有說我們是殺牛人，因為我們宰殺過牛。
(They) only call us people who kill oxen, because we used to kill oxen.

S.FORM: mesua ia lipuung sua ni nimikaarU KANa kanavu.
S.TRANSL: 那時，（其實）是日本人叫卡那卡那富去殺牛的。
Then, (actually) it was Japanese who made KANa kanavu kill the oxen.

S.FORM: cucuru necU'Ure mesua KANa kanavu ia nipaliku pakisia.
S.TRANSL: 真的，你看，當時的卡那卡那富被閩南人追趕。
Really, you look, KANa kanavu at that time was chased by the south Fujian people.

S.FORM: nipaliku pakisia mesua palikusa la'ititaana.
S.TRANSL: 被趕到懸崖區的上面。
(We) were chased to the area of cliffs.

S.FORM: UU sua maratavarU'U ia 'inmamia sua makai mamarang miana ia.
S.TRANSL: 比較了解的，只有像以前的老人家。
The ones understanding better are, for example, old persons in the past.

S.FORM: 'ia'ah makasia nava pakisia iisi.
S.TRANSL: 「哇！這個閩南人原來是這樣。」
"Wow! So that's how the south Fujian people are like."

S.FORM: 'una nimuca makasi tuunpe itUitaan.
S.TRANSL: 有（人）去Tuunpe那邊的懸崖。
There were (people) going to the cliff areas of Tuunpe.

S.FORM: umovici tarisi tiree nia putUnUkUe itaan.
S.TRANSL: 他帶了繩索，把那個??在懸崖區的??。
He brought a rope and ?? that at the ?? of the cliff areas.

S.FORM: palikuun cu kan mamarang kanakanavu sua pakisia.
S.TRANSL: 卡那卡那富的老人家去追閩南人。
The Kanakanavu old people chased the south Fujian people.

S.FORM: mucan cu kan pakisia tia matisa'U na tarisi tia mo'UtU'Utaan.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人逃跑了，要抓繩索，想要通過懸崖。
The south Fujian people escaped, wanted to grip.

S.FORM: 'esi cu kan vuvucUUke posUrUUtUn kan mamarang tarisi.
S.TRANSL: （當）他們在中間（的時候），老人家砍斷繩索。
(When) they were at the middle, the old people cut the rope.

S.FORM: makai kan nimiurUU'U kan maa sua pakasia matU'Une UnU'Unee.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人就像??一樣，（掉到）地面。
The south Fujian people (fell) downward to the ground like ??.

S.FORM: makasua sua kari iisi.
S.TRANSL: 這個傳說是那個樣子。
This legend is like that.

S.FORM: mata 'inmamia sua 'Avia 'Uva ia 'esi kan tinpurui kan kumakUUn UU neen taana samunhi niara nimara kan ma'ain.
S.TRANSL: 另外，據說只有'Avia 'Uva在坐著吃，拿了叫做??的東西和鹽巴。
Moreover, it is said only 'Avia 'Uva sat eating, taking something called ?? and salt.

S.FORM: 'una kan cau cinimUra nia.
S.TRANSL: 有人看過他。
There were people having seen him.

S.FORM: sii mia makasua makasua ci ia nimoon UU 'una sua pakasia nimoon kan ngaca'U to'omaan nipukingti 'esi kan nia uncoo nimukUru kan pangarU.
S.TRANSL: 因為當那樣之後，曾經有閩南人在刺竹的根部那裡握著矛。
Because after it was that way, there used to be south Fujian people holding spears at the root of thorny bamboo.

S.FORM: potintiti ci kan na nacio to'ona makasua kani.
S.TRANSL: （他們）在??的地方那樣??。
(They) ?? at the place of ?? like that.

S.FORM: kan motUrUUsU kan mamia ngaca'U to'ona.
S.TRANSL: 根部那個地方只有??。
At the root was only ??.

S.FORM: apitUan maa nipukingti.
S.TRANSL: 那個刺竹只有??。
That thorny bamboo was only ??.

S.FORM: umanguru cu sua kana kanakanavu mi mana ma'icUpU.
S.TRANSL: The Kanakanavu (people) escaped, perhaps (because they) were scared.
卡那卡那富逃走了，可能是（因為）害怕吧。

S.FORM: iimo sua kari iisi ia sua iikaam tinmana ia timanoo ku kari iisi masi ka'aan kaam ruem kari iisi.
S.TRANSL: 所以這些你們聽過的話，要聽我的話才不會忘記。
Therefore, as for these words, which you have heard, listen to me so that you will not forget them.

S.FORM: makasua kari maku kamua masitian tumaturu mamaan tati'ing.
S.TRANSL: 我跟你們說的那個樣子，叮嚀著告訴後輩們。
I exhort and tell the children with what I have told you.

S.FORM: arasingsinga mamanUnga tina'an sosomaanpe curu kamua.
S.TRANSL: 祝福你們，讓你們有好的身體，非常感謝你們。
May you be blessed, may you have a good body, thank you very much.

S.FORM: arasingsinga.
S.TRANSL: 祝福你們。
May you be blessed.
